word: G4847 - συμπολίτης - sympolítēs - soom-pol-ee'-tace - Fellow- citizen

sections.paragraph:
- Original: συμπολίτης

- Transliteration: Sumpolites

- Phonetic: soom-pol-ee'-tace

- Definition:

1. possessing the same citizenship with others, a fellow citizen

a. of Gentiles as received into communion of the saints

b. of the people consecrated to God

- Origin: from G4862 and G4177

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: From G4862 and G4177; a native of the same town that is (figuratively) co-religionist (fellow Christian): - fellow-citizen.
